title: Java Initializer Blocks
description: Practice static and instance initializer execution before constructors finish.
learningGoals: Trace static initializer execution once when the class is initialized.
Trace instance initializer execution before the constructor body for each object.

completionChecks.prompt: What does new Account("Mira").label() return for the first Account object created?
completionChecks.explanation: The static initializer sets nextId to 100 once. The instance initializer assigns id before the constructor stores the owner, so label later returns Mira#100.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens to the instance initializer block?
choices: It runs once when the class is loaded
It runs before the constructor body for each new Account
It runs only when label() is called
It cannot assign to final fields
completionChecks.explanation: Instance initializer blocks are copied into each constructor after super() and field initializers, so they run for every new object before the constructor body completes.

completionChecks.prompt: What does a second new Account("Noor").label() return after the first Account was Mira#100?
completionChecks.explanation: The static initializer does not run again. The first instance consumed id 100, so the next instance initializer assigns id 101 before the Noor constructor body stores the owner.
